subject: Public Health
title: Health Indicators and Rankings
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Health Indicators and Rankings.

hook.text: How do you rank a country's health? Not by the number of hospitals—by outcomes.

buildup.text: Infant mortality, maternal death rates, and vaccination coverage are key indicators.

discovery.text: The Human Development Index combines health, education, and income into one score per country.

twist.text: The US spends more per person on health than any nation but ranks below 30 others on life expectancy.

climax.text: Spending more doesn't guarantee better health—how you spend matters far more.

punchline.text: The best health systems measure outcomes, not budgets.

quiz.question: Why does the US rank poorly on life expectancy despite high spending?
options: How money is spent matters more than how much
